certification.title: ATEX and IECEx Certification: Notified Bodies, Test Reports, Quality Systems
certification.description: How explosion-protected equipment gets certified. Notified Bodies, IECEx scheme, ExTR test reports, quality audits, and CompEx competence.

cheat-sheet.title: Ex Certification Cheat Sheet: Quick Reference for Engineers
cheat-sheet.description: Six qualification questions, zone-to-EPL mapping, protection method decision tree, certification cost and timeline estimates, common mistakes.

epl.title: Equipment Protection Levels: Ga, Gb, Gc, Da, Db, Dc Explained
epl.description: Equipment Protection Levels Ga, Gb, Gc for gas and Da, Db, Dc for dust. How EPL connects zone classification to protection method selection.

ex-markings.title: ATEX and IECEx Marking Guide: How to Read Ex Equipment Labels
ex-markings.description: Decode ATEX and IECEx markings step by step. Five real-world examples walked through piece by piece. Group, zone, temperature class, EPL.

faq.title: FAQ — Explosion Protection, ATEX, IECEx Questions Answered
faq.description: Frequently asked questions about ATEX certification, IECEx, zone classification, Ex markings, equipment protection levels, and explosion protection standards.

fundamentals.title: Explosion Protection Fundamentals: Fire Triangle, LEL/UEL, Ignition Sources
fundamentals.description: The fire triangle, explosive atmospheres, LEL and UEL limits, 13 ignition sources, and the three protection principles. A practical guide for engineers.

gas-groups.title: Gas Groups IIA, IIB, IIC and Dust Groups IIIA, IIIB, IIIC Explained
gas-groups.description: Gas groups IIA, IIB, IIC explained with MESG and MIC ratio. Dust groups IIIA, IIIB, IIIC. Hierarchy rules and NEC equivalents.

installation-inspection.title: Ex Equipment Installation and Inspection: IEC 60079-14 and 60079-17 Guide
installation-inspection.description: Cable glands, clearance distances, three inspection grades, ten common mistakes, per IEC 60079-14 and 60079-17 requirements.

protection-methods.title: Ex Protection Methods: Ex d, Ex e, Ex i, Ex p, Ex n, Ex m and More
protection-methods.description: All explosion protection methods in one place. Flameproof (Ex d), intrinsic safety (Ex i), increased safety (Ex e), pressurization (Ex p), and more.

standards.title: Explosion Protection Standards: IEC 60079, ATEX Directives, NEC 500/505
standards.description: The full IEC 60079 series, ATEX 2014/34/EU and 1999/92/EC, NEC 500/505, CEC, and regional standards from Brazil to Australia.

temperature-classes.title: Temperature Classes T1-T6: Auto-Ignition Temperatures for Ex Equipment
temperature-classes.description: Temperature classes T1 through T6, auto-ignition temperatures, dust layer ignition, and how to select the right T-class for your equipment.

zone-classification.title: ATEX Zone Classification: Zone 0, 1, 2 (Gas) and Zone 20, 21, 22 (Dust)
zone-classification.description: How hazardous areas are classified into Zones 0, 1, 2 for gas and 20, 21, 22 for dust. Methodology, IEC 60079-10, examples, and common mistakes.
